## 2026-04-18 06:36 — US-048
- Added SECURITY INVOKER SQL function `public.lister_dashboard_counts()` returning three integer columns (`active_campaigns`, `pending_applications`, `pending_submissions`) via three caller-scoped sub-selects joined on `lister_id = public.current_user_id()`.
- Replaced the `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` US-031 placeholder with a real dashboard: three neubrutalist StatTile cards, SkeletonCard loading, pull-to-refresh, error copy, and the design-doc §5 empty-state "Post your first bounty." when all three counts are zero.
- Wired the existing Fab to `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1')` (route lands in US-049; tapping currently resolves to expo-router `+not-found`).
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` (rewrote placeholder)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — new `lister_dashboard_counts` entry in Functions)
  - `scripts/test/probe-us048-dashboard-counts.ts` (new end-to-end probe)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-048-dashboard-error-state.png`
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-048-fab-navigates.png`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. Medium: (1) `allowFontScaling={false}` on the hero stat number is the wrong opt-out — per design.md §1.3 + §11 checklist, content text uses `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; UI chrome (pills, chips, tab labels) uses `allowFontScaling={false}`. Swapped both the value and label Text to `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. (2) Missing empty state — design.md §5 names "Post your first bounty." as the dashboard-empty copy. Added `isAllZero(counts)` gate that renders the empty message instead of three "0" tiles for a new lister. Also addressed the code-reviewer's observation about the unauthed-user loading edge (matches the reference applications.tsx pattern, acceptable).
- Spec gap: AC names the file `app/(lister)/index.tsx`, but the canonical route per docs/design.md §2.3 is `/(lister)/dashboard` and the file already lives at `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` (US-031). Specs win; overwrote the placeholder rather than creating `index.tsx`. Noted in the file header comment.
- Mobile-mcp verification: sim iPhone 17 Pro iOS 26.3. Forced fresh auth per Pattern #100 (`mobile_terminate_app` → `xcrun simctl keychain reset` → `mobile_launch_app`). Role picker → DEV BYPASS → "Sign in as lister". Error-state screenshot captured (dev fake JWT fails verification — Pattern #100) showing every structural element: header, error copy, FAB at bottom-right, custom TabBar with HOME active. Tapped FAB → expo-router `+not-found` (route lands in US-049) — second screenshot confirms `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1')` fires correctly. Happy path verified two ways: (a) transactional SQL test earlier (seeded acme lister returns `{2,2,1}`, unknown UUID returns `{0,0,0}`); (b) `probe-us048-dashboard-counts.ts` — signs up a fresh lister via `auth-signup-lister` → calls `/rest/v1/rpc/lister_dashboard_counts` with the real Marketify JWT → asserts 200 + `[{0,0,0}]` shape. Probe pass proves the full PostgREST → JWT verify → RPC → type-coercion path works end-to-end; test lister row deleted via service_role after the probe.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Dashboard-style RPC that exposes `count(*)` aggregates per caller-scoped filter — prefer a single SECURITY INVOKER SQL function returning a typed row over N PostgREST `.select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })` round-trips. Three wins: (1) atomic — the three numbers come from the same snapshot; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller gets column names; (3) one network hop. Belt-and-suspenders `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` inside every sub-select even though RLS already applies — a future RLS regression would otherwise silently inflate counts with other callers' rows.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Design.md §1.3 scaling rule has a clear cutline — content text uses `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` (Dynamic Type honored up to 130%); UI chrome (pills, chips, tab-bar labels) uses `allowFontScaling={false}`. Hero metric numbers ARE content even when they look like UI, so they get the multiplier cap. Easy to get wrong on "display"-size Text that happens to sit inside a tile.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Empty-state copy in design.md §5 is canon — the "all zeroes" render of a counts-dashboard is NOT "show 0 / 0 / 0 in tiles", it's the screen-specific empty message from §5. Treat "zero data" and "empty state" as distinct UI states even when they share the underlying data shape.
  - When a UI story depends on a route that doesn't yet exist (here: US-049's wizard), wire the router.push to the target path anyway — expo-router's `+not-found` is a graceful fallback and the nav starts working the instant the downstream file lands. Don't leave a toast-placeholder behind; it'll be forgotten when the target ships.
  - Verification recipe for a dashboard RPC under a real JWT: `auth-signup-<role>` → capture `token` from the 200 body → POST `/rest/v1/rpc/<name>` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and the anon key as `apikey` → assert shape + values. Cleanup via `DELETE /rest/v1/<table>?id=eq.<uuid>` under service_role, or `execute_sql` through the MCP if the service key isn't in the probe env. Pattern reused from US-043.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Dashboard counts RPC pattern; Font-scaling cutline rule; Zero-counts-as-empty-state distinction.

## 2026-04-18 06:43 — US-044 (re-verify attempt, still blocked)
- No code changes. Carry-over from prior iteration: implementation + code-review complete; only the AC's E2E mobile-mcp/`execute_sql` flip-and-confirm verification was outstanding because Supabase Realtime's tenant cold-start was failing.
- Re-ran the probe end-to-end: `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup` → minted real Marketify JWT for user `7ca930b8-…2297`; seeded a `pending` application via `execute_sql` (id `1d5d3800-…6350`, listing seed `11111111-…010`); ran `subscribe` mode → broker returned `status=TIMED_OUT` four times then `[verdict] FAIL` after the 60s window. Same failure shape as previous iteration.
- `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` confirms the postgres_changes path is still broken: latest entries `InitializingProjectConnection` (14:21:18 UTC) → `UnableToConnectToProject` (14:21:37) → `UnableToConnectToProject` (14:21:56). Earlier today there was a successful warm-up (`Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 476 ms`) but only the `supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (broadcast) slot started — the `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes) slot never appears in 24h of logs. Two prior CheckConnection runs that day landed at 672 s (~11 min) and 800 s (~13 min). The cold-start path for the postgres_changes replication slot specifically is the broken layer; broadcast appears to recover.
- Test fixtures cleaned up immediately via `execute_sql` (`delete from public.applications where id = …`, then `delete from public.users where id = …` — `users` cascade clears the auth-signup-creator side rows).
- Quality checks: tsc n/a (no code change) / lint n/a / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (probe is the AC-specified verification path)
- Code-reviewer verdict: n/a (no code change)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The two Realtime replication slots are independent failure domains: `supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (broadcast/messages) and `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes). When debugging postgres_changes subscriptions on this project, `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` showing the messages slot warm up is NOT sufficient — explicitly grep for the postgres_changes slot name. If only messages appears, the `applications`/`submissions` UPDATE stream cannot be delivered even though the tenant looks alive.
  - The `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` metric is the single-most-useful health signal for this tenant. <1 s = healthy, 100s of seconds = degraded cold-start, eventual `Tenant has no connected users` after a window of inactivity = back to cold and the next subscriber will pay the cost again. Pattern is consistent across multiple iterations now — likely a Supabase free-tier hibernation interaction with this region (ap-southeast-1).
  - Probe convention works: `signup` mode mints a real JWT; the operator seeds a row via `execute_sql`; `subscribe` mode subscribes + waits 60 s; cleanup via `execute_sql delete` (cascade through `public.users` clears everything). Reusable pattern for any future story that needs a real-JWT-only realtime/RLS verification.

  - RN 0.81's sticky footer hides content behind it when the ScrollView contentInsetAdjustmentBehavior isn't tuned — in this composer the 2 post-condition rows both fit above the Send CTA on iPhone 17 Pro but the second is covered by the sticky button; a short swipe-up inside the scroll area reveals it. For screens with more than ~2 rows or smaller devices, add `paddingBottom: 96` to the ScrollView contentContainerStyle so content scrolls above the footer.
  - INCOMPLETE_AFFIRMATIONS is the server's way of signaling "the post-condition set has drifted since you loaded the composer." The client must distinguish two sub-cases by comparing the returned `missing_condition_ids` to the local `state.conditions` id Set: (a) every missing id is locally known → user actually didn't tick something (tick-per-id + toast), (b) any missing id isn't locally known → listing version changed mid-composer (toast + force reload). Inlining this in `onSubmit` keeps the logic close to the state being patched and avoids passing `setAffirmations` through a shared helper.
  - Error-decode pattern on `supabase.functions.invoke`: the `error` object has a `context?: Response` whose `.json()` returns the edge function's `{error, ...}` body. Always narrow via `(error as {context?: Response}).context` — the supabase-js type doesn't expose it, but it's stable across v2.x.
  - Test fixture gotcha: the seeded approved application belongs to `seed_creator`, but the simulator's dev-picker creator is a per-session user. For any composer-style story, insert a fresh approved application for the currently-signed-in simulator user before opening the composer screen (or pick a listing + version pair whose conditions match the story's AC). Reference fixture used here: listing `22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222010` / version `22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222011` has 2 post-conditions (family_friendly + must_mention "#LuxeDrop") — good for verifying the multi-row checklist path.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) client/server oEmbed mirror pattern — keep `supabase/functions/_shared/oembed.ts` and `src/lib/oembed.ts` in sync manually; the server is authoritative and the client preview is cosmetic.

## 2026-04-18 06:30 — US-047
- Creator "My Submissions" tab at `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx`: three-segment control (pending / approved / rejected) mirroring the US-043 applied tab's shape, calling a new SECURITY DEFINER RPC `public.list_my_submissions()` that joins submissions → applications → listings → users (lister handle) → listing_versions (snapshot title fallback) + a lateral `submission_videos` pick-first. Row card routes to the listing detail (submissions have no dedicated detail screen in v1).
- Realtime subscription on `public.submissions` UPDATE events with NO server-side filter — submissions has no `creator_id` column, so the RLS policy `submissions_creator_rw` (join-through-applications) is the security boundary, same pattern as the REST read path. `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` flow matches US-044 verbatim; the handler patches `status` in-place via a `rowsRef` snapshot so a rejected→approved flip jumps segments without a manual refresh.
- Publication membership added via `alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.submissions` (default replica identity suffices — payload only reads `.new.id` + `.new.status`).
- Spec gap logged: AC names the file `submitted.tsx` but US-031's TabBar routes to `/(creator)/submissions`. Kept the filename to preserve the routing convention; header renders "Submitted" per docs/design.md §3.1 tab label.
- US-044 re-probe: ran `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup` + subscribe with a fresh user — still TIMED_OUT twice. Tenant CheckConnection improved (476ms vs earlier 800322ms) but subscribe still never reaches SUBSCRIBED. Infra unchanged; US-044 stays `passes:false` per prior iteration's decision rule. No code touched.
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418050000_us_047_list_my_submissions_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418050100_us_047_submissions_realtime_publication.sql` (new)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — adds `list_my_submissions` Function entry)
  - `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` (rewritten from TabPlaceholder stub to full screen)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-047-pending-real.png` (new — real-session populated pending row)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-047-approved-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-047-rejected-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-047 `passes:true` + Spec gap notes)
  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (SECURITY DEFINER with empty search_path + schema-qualified refs, no new findings) / mobile-mcp ✓ (all three segments screenshotted; pending segment even shows a real submission card from a previously-authed creator session, which unexpectedly proves the RPC path end-to-end beyond the planned empty-state verification)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. No blocking issues. Noted the same stale-token capture pattern as US-036/US-044 (out of scope — consistent across realtime screens, to be addressed systematically if/when the custom-JWT wrapper gets a refresh hook).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Realtime `postgres_changes` WITHOUT a server-side filter is the correct pattern when the target table has no direct caller-scoping column; RLS does the gating server-side, and omitting the filter clause is required — passing e.g. `filter: 'application_id=in.(...)'` would require the client to pre-fetch + maintain the application-id set, re-subscribe on every new application, and race against the RLS evaluation. Cheaper + more correct to rely on `submissions_creator_rw`. Same posture would apply to any future "child table gated via a join" (submission_videos updates, review_threads, etc.).
  - `submission_status` only has three values (pending / approved / rejected) — no cancellation variants to collapse, so the screen does NOT need the `STATUS_BUCKET` Record indirection US-043's applied tab uses. Direct `rows.filter(r => r.status === segment)` is sufficient. Keep this per-screen: the bucket map is only required when the DB enum is richer than the UI segments.
  - Sibling-screen parity: re-implementing the same SegmentControl + card + empty-state shape for US-047 mirrored applications.tsx 1:1 except for the RPC name, the enum set, and the realtime filter omission. Copying-with-light-edits is the right tradeoff over premature abstraction — a shared `<SegmentedList>` primitive doesn't pay off until a third screen needs the same pattern (lister-side applications or submissions list could be the trigger).
  - Mobile-mcp unexpectedly found a real submission during verification (from a prior-session authenticated creator). This validated the RPC end-to-end instead of just verifying empty-state — a stronger check than planned. When a persisted session renders a non-empty state, lean into it for verification rather than tearing down to reach the empty state.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Realtime subscription without server-side filter when the table lacks a direct caller-id column; rely on RLS.

## 2026-04-18 06:36 — US-048
- Added SECURITY INVOKER SQL function `public.lister_dashboard_counts()` returning three integer columns (`active_campaigns`, `pending_applications`, `pending_submissions`) via three caller-scoped sub-selects joined on `lister_id = public.current_user_id()`.
- Replaced the `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` US-031 placeholder with a real dashboard: three neubrutalist StatTile cards, SkeletonCard loading, pull-to-refresh, error copy, and the design-doc §5 empty-state "Post your first bounty." when all three counts are zero.
- Wired the existing Fab to `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1')` (route lands in US-049; tapping currently resolves to expo-router `+not-found`).
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` (rewrote placeholder)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — new `lister_dashboard_counts` entry in Functions)
  - `scripts/test/probe-us048-dashboard-counts.ts` (new end-to-end probe)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-048-dashboard-error-state.png`
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-048-fab-navigates.png`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. Medium: (1) `allowFontScaling={false}` on the hero stat number is the wrong opt-out — per design.md §1.3 + §11 checklist, content text uses `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; UI chrome (pills, chips, tab labels) uses `allowFontScaling={false}`. Swapped both the value and label Text to `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. (2) Missing empty state — design.md §5 names "Post your first bounty." as the dashboard-empty copy. Added `isAllZero(counts)` gate that renders the empty message instead of three "0" tiles for a new lister. Also addressed the code-reviewer's observation about the unauthed-user loading edge (matches the reference applications.tsx pattern, acceptable).
- Spec gap: AC names the file `app/(lister)/index.tsx`, but the canonical route per docs/design.md §2.3 is `/(lister)/dashboard` and the file already lives at `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` (US-031). Specs win; overwrote the placeholder rather than creating `index.tsx`. Noted in the file header comment.
- Mobile-mcp verification: sim iPhone 17 Pro iOS 26.3. Forced fresh auth per Pattern #100 (`mobile_terminate_app` → `xcrun simctl keychain reset` → `mobile_launch_app`). Role picker → DEV BYPASS → "Sign in as lister". Error-state screenshot captured (dev fake JWT fails verification — Pattern #100) showing every structural element: header, error copy, FAB at bottom-right, custom TabBar with HOME active. Tapped FAB → expo-router `+not-found` (route lands in US-049) — second screenshot confirms `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1')` fires correctly. Happy path verified two ways: (a) transactional SQL test earlier (seeded acme lister returns `{2,2,1}`, unknown UUID returns `{0,0,0}`); (b) `probe-us048-dashboard-counts.ts` — signs up a fresh lister via `auth-signup-lister` → calls `/rest/v1/rpc/lister_dashboard_counts` with the real Marketify JWT → asserts 200 + `[{0,0,0}]` shape. Probe pass proves the full PostgREST → JWT verify → RPC → type-coercion path works end-to-end; test lister row deleted via service_role after the probe.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Dashboard-style RPC that exposes `count(*)` aggregates per caller-scoped filter — prefer a single SECURITY INVOKER SQL function returning a typed row over N PostgREST `.select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })` round-trips. Three wins: (1) atomic — the three numbers come from the same snapshot; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller gets column names; (3) one network hop. Belt-and-suspenders `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` inside every sub-select even though RLS already applies — a future RLS regression would otherwise silently inflate counts with other callers' rows.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Design.md §1.3 scaling rule has a clear cutline — content text uses `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` (Dynamic Type honored up to 130%); UI chrome (pills, chips, tab-bar labels) uses `allowFontScaling={false}`. Hero metric numbers ARE content even when they look like UI, so they get the multiplier cap. Easy to get wrong on "display"-size Text that happens to sit inside a tile.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Empty-state copy in design.md §5 is canon — the "all zeroes" render of a counts-dashboard is NOT "show 0 / 0 / 0 in tiles", it's the screen-specific empty message from §5. Treat "zero data" and "empty state" as distinct UI states even when they share the underlying data shape.
  - When a UI story depends on a route that doesn't yet exist (here: US-049's wizard), wire the router.push to the target path anyway — expo-router's `+not-found` is a graceful fallback and the nav starts working the instant the downstream file lands. Don't leave a toast-placeholder behind; it'll be forgotten when the target ships.
  - Verification recipe for a dashboard RPC under a real JWT: `auth-signup-<role>` → capture `token` from the 200 body → POST `/rest/v1/rpc/<name>` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and the anon key as `apikey` → assert shape + values. Cleanup via `DELETE /rest/v1/<table>?id=eq.<uuid>` under service_role, or `execute_sql` through the MCP if the service key isn't in the probe env. Pattern reused from US-043.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Dashboard counts RPC pattern; Font-scaling cutline rule; Zero-counts-as-empty-state distinction.

## 2026-04-18 06:43 — US-044 (re-verify attempt, still blocked)
- No code changes. Carry-over from prior iteration: implementation + code-review complete; only the AC's E2E mobile-mcp/`execute_sql` flip-and-confirm verification was outstanding because Supabase Realtime's tenant cold-start was failing.
- Re-ran the probe end-to-end: `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup` → minted real Marketify JWT for user `7ca930b8-…2297`; seeded a `pending` application via `execute_sql` (id `1d5d3800-…6350`, listing seed `11111111-…010`); ran `subscribe` mode → broker returned `status=TIMED_OUT` four times then `[verdict] FAIL` after the 60s window. Same failure shape as previous iteration.
- `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` confirms the postgres_changes path is still broken: latest entries `InitializingProjectConnection` (14:21:18 UTC) → `UnableToConnectToProject` (14:21:37) → `UnableToConnectToProject` (14:21:56). Earlier today there was a successful warm-up (`Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 476 ms`) but only the `supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (broadcast) slot started — the `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes) slot never appears in 24h of logs. Two prior CheckConnection runs that day landed at 672 s (~11 min) and 800 s (~13 min). The cold-start path for the postgres_changes replication slot specifically is the broken layer; broadcast appears to recover.
- Test fixtures cleaned up immediately via `execute_sql` (`delete from public.applications where id = …`, then `delete from public.users where id = …` — `users` cascade clears the auth-signup-creator side rows).
- Quality checks: tsc n/a (no code change) / lint n/a / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (probe is the AC-specified verification path)
- Code-reviewer verdict: n/a (no code change)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The two Realtime replication slots are independent failure domains: `supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (broadcast/messages) and `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes). When debugging postgres_changes subscriptions on this project, `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` showing the messages slot warm up is NOT sufficient — explicitly grep for the postgres_changes slot name. If only messages appears, the `applications`/`submissions` UPDATE stream cannot be delivered even though the tenant looks alive.
  - The `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` metric is the single-most-useful health signal for this tenant. <1 s = healthy, 100s of seconds = degraded cold-start, eventual `Tenant has no connected users` after a window of inactivity = back to cold and the next subscriber will pay the cost again. Pattern is consistent across multiple iterations now — likely a Supabase free-tier hibernation interaction with this region (ap-southeast-1).
  - Probe convention works: `signup` mode mints a real JWT; the operator seeds a row via `execute_sql`; `subscribe` mode subscribes + waits 60 s; cleanup via `execute_sql delete` (cascade through `public.users` clears everything). Reusable pattern for any future story that needs a real-JWT-only realtime/RLS verification.

content.content:
"id": "US-044",
      "title": "Realtime subscription for application status changes (creator)",
      "description": "Push status flips into the UI via Supabase Realtime.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Subscribe to applications WHERE creator_user_id = me on the Applied tab",
        "On UPDATE: optimistically move the row to the new segment; fire toast 'Your application was approved/rejected'",
        "Mobile-mcp verification: from Supabase Studio (or via execute_sql) flip a seeded application to approved while the screen is open — confirm UI updates without manual refresh",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 44,
      "passes": false,
      "notes": "2026-04-18: Implementation complete + code-reviewed, but E2E realtime delivery verification BLOCKED by Supabase Realtime tenant infrastructure failure for project [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]. Server-side realtime logs show persistent 'UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database' every 5-15s (even after the probe stops), with one prior 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 494396 ms' (~8min) suggesting a stuck tenant cold-start path — this is beyond what the client can recover from. Work shipped this iteration: (1) migration us_044_applications_realtime_publication adds public.applications to supabase_realtime (default replica identity is sufficient because the client only reads payload.new.id+status); (2) app/(creator)/applications.tsx extended with a useEffect that calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()) then subscribes to UPDATE events on applications filtered by creator_id=eq.<me>, patches rows in state on a status change, and fires the AC-specified success/error toast ('Your application was approved/rejected') for approved and rejected only (other 4 enum values patch the row silently since AC only names the two decisions); (3) a useRef mirror of `rows` (Codebase Pattern #82/#106) prevents stale-closure reads inside the subscription callback without forcing re-subscription on every list mutation; (4) mobile-mcp captured us-044-applied-pending-empty.png + us-044-applied-approved-empty.png proving the subscription wiring doesn't crash the screen under the DevRolePicker creator session (subscription is no-op there because the dev token is a literal string, not a signed JWT — same posture as US-036/US-043 per Codebase Pattern #122); (5) scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts two-mode probe (signup | subscribe) that mints a real Marketify JWT via auth-signup-creator, calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(jwt), subscribes to UPDATE events under the same RLS policy (applications_creator_rw, polcmd='*') that guards the in-app stream, and asserts payload.new.status matches expected — ran end-to-end this iteration and returned FAIL with '[channel] status=TIMED_OUT' repeated four times because the broker never reached SUBSCRIBED. Probe test fixture (user_id a3495acd-7241-4d41-8e15-d42a210fdf65 + cascading rows) was cleaned up immediately via execute_sql. Code-reviewer returned 2 IMPORTANT findings, both carried over unchanged from US-036 (stale-token-at-subscription-time baseline across all realtime screens) and both AC-conformant (toast fires only on approved/rejected, matching the exact AC wording). Next iteration should re-run the probe (bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup then subscribe) once Supabase Realtime tenant is healthy — if verdict PASS, flip passes:true. Tenant hibernation/cold-start is the likely root cause; waiting for the tenant to warm up + checking get_logs({service:'realtime'}) for 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (the postgres_changes slot, not just the messages slot) before running the probe is the recommended order. | 2026-04-18 06:43 re-verify: realtime tenant still broken — same TIMED_OUT pattern. New probe run signed up user 7ca930b8-...2297, seeded application 1d5d3800-...6350 against listing 11111111-...010, subscribed → 4x TIMED_OUT then verdict FAIL. get_logs({service:realtime}) confirms only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 ever starts; the postgres_changes slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2 has not appeared in 24h. CheckConnection latency oscillates 476ms (healthy) ↔ 670s+ (cold-start) ↔ tenant-shutdown-on-idle. No code change this iteration; fixtures cleaned up. Next iteration: re-run the probe as before; if get_logs shows the postgres_changes slot starting (search for slot name without the messages_ infix), the broker is finally healthy and the probe should reach SUBSCRIBED."
    },

content.content:
1	import { Redirect, Slot } from 'expo-router';
2	import { Building2, Inbox, LayoutDashboard, Megaphone } from 'lucide-react-native';
3	import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
4	import { TabBar, type TabDef } from '@/components/shared/TabBar';
5	import { colors } from '@/design/tokens';
6	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
7	
8	// Lister tab group — Home (index) / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile per US-031 AC
9	// + docs/design.md §3.2. Same custom-TabBar rationale as the creator layout:
10	// expo-router's native `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch. The FAB
11	// is mounted on the Home screen per §3.2 ("Create-campaign is a FAB on
12	// Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump").
13	const LISTER_TABS: TabDef[] = [
14	  {
15	    label: 'Home',
16	    icon: LayoutDashboard,
17	    path: '/(lister)/dashboard',
18	    activePathname: '/dashboard',
19	    testID: 'tab-dashboard',
20	  },
21	  {
22	    label: 'Campaigns',
23	    icon: Megaphone,
24	    path: '/(lister)/campaigns',
25	    activePathname: '/campaigns',
26	    testID: 'tab-campaigns',
27	  },
28	  {
29	    label: 'Inbox',
30	    icon: Inbox,
31	    path: '/(lister)/inbox',
32	    activePathname: '/inbox',
33	    testID: 'tab-inbox',
34	  },
35	  {
36	    label: 'Profile',
37	    icon: Building2,
38	    path: '/(lister)/profile',
39	    activePathname: '/profile',
40	    testID: 'tab-profile',
41	  },
42	];
43	
44	export default function ListerLayout() {
45	  const { role } = useAuth();
46	  if (role !== 'lister') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
47	
48	  return (
49	    <View style={styles.root}>
50	      <View style={styles.content}>
51	        <Slot />
52	      </View>
53	      <TabBar tabs={LISTER_TABS} />
54	    </View>
55	  );
56	}
57	
58	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
59	  root: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: colors.canvas },
60	  content: { flex: 1 },
61	});
62

content.content:
1	// Typography — typed text-style presets for the Marketify design system.
2	// Mirrors docs/design.md §1.3 (font pairing table + line-height rules).
3	//
4	// Font substitution: docs/design.md names Clash Display (headings) and Satoshi (body).
5	// Both are Fontshare-hosted and do not ship with Expo Google Fonts; we use the explicit
6	// fallbacks listed in §1.3 — Outfit for display/headings, Rubik for body/caption — plus
7	// JetBrains Mono for monospace. Spec-sanctioned per the same section.
8	//
9	// `fontFamily` values are the string keys registered with `expo-font.useFonts` in
10	// `app/_layout.tsx`. They MUST match the constant names imported from
11	// `@expo-google-fonts/*` so RN can resolve the loaded assets by name.
12	
13	import {
14	  Outfit_600SemiBold,
15	  Outfit_700Bold,
16	} from '@expo-google-fonts/outfit';
17	import {
18	  Rubik_500Medium,
19	  Rubik_700Bold,
20	} from '@expo-google-fonts/rubik';
21	import { JetBrainsMono_500Medium } from '@expo-google-fonts/jetbrains-mono';
22	import type { TextStyle } from 'react-native';
23	
24	export const fontFamilies = {
25	  displayBold: 'Outfit_700Bold',
26	  displaySemi: 'Outfit_600SemiBold',
27	  bodyMedium: 'Rubik_500Medium',
28	  bodyBold: 'Rubik_700Bold',
29	  mono: 'JetBrainsMono_500Medium',
30	} as const;
31	
32	// Map consumed by `useFonts` — values are the numeric asset handles exported by
33	// @expo-google-fonts/*, keys are the runtime font-family names.
34	export const fontAssets = {
35	  [fontFamilies.displayBold]: Outfit_700Bold,
36	  [fontFamilies.displaySemi]: Outfit_600SemiBold,
37	  [fontFamilies.bodyMedium]: Rubik_500Medium,
38	  [fontFamilies.bodyBold]: Rubik_700Bold,
39	  [fontFamilies.mono]: JetBrainsMono_500Medium,
40	} as const;
41	
42	// Line heights per §1.3: 1.15 for display, ~1.55 for body. Caption + mono tighten
43	// to 1.4 for dense data rows.
44	export const textStyles = {
45	  display: {
46	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displayBold,
47	    fontSize: 28,
48	    lineHeight: 32,
49	  },
50	  h1: {
51	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displaySemi,
52	    fontSize: 22,
53	    lineHeight: 25,
54	  },
55	  h2: {
56	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displaySemi,
57	    fontSize: 20,
58	    lineHeight: 23,
59	  },
60	  body: {
61	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyMedium,
62	    fontSize: 15,
63	    lineHeight: 23,
64	  },
65	  caption: {
66	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyMedium,
67	    fontSize: 13,
68	    lineHeight: 18,
69	  },
70	  mono: {
71	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.mono,
72	    fontSize: 14,
73	    lineHeight: 20,
74	  },
75	  // Micro (pills + chip labels) per §1.3 — Rubik_700Bold stands in for Satoshi 700,
76	  // 0.08em tracking at 11pt = 0.88pt (RN letterSpacing is absolute points).
77	  micro: {
78	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.bodyBold,
79	    fontSize: 11,
80	    lineHeight: 14,
81	    letterSpacing: 0.88,
82	    textTransform: 'uppercase',
83	  },
84	} as const satisfies Record<string, TextStyle>;
85	
86	export type TextStyleToken = keyof typeof textStyles;
87	export type FontFamilyToken = keyof typeof fontFamilies;
88

input.prompt:
Review the implementation of Marketify US-049 — Create-campaign wizard step 1 (basics). This is running inside the Ralph autonomous loop for the Marketify project.

## Story acceptance criteria (from scripts/ralph/prd.json)

US-049 — Create-campaign wizard step 1 — basics
- "app/(lister)/create/step-1.tsx with title, description, price (number), currency (USD/EUR/GBP)"
- "Stores draft in zustand or react-context wizard store + MMKV"
- "Next button enabled when title + description present"
- "Mobile-mcp verification: fill, navigate next, screenshot"
- "Commit"

## What was implemented

Three new files:

1. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` — React Context + AsyncStorage persistence for the full wizard draft shape (title, description, priceCents, currency, preConditions, postConditions, sampleUrls, maxSubmissions). Debounced persist (250 ms). Hydrates once on mount and gates on `hydrated` boolean. Defensive `isWizardDraftShape` guard on rehydration so a corrupted / schema-drifted payload doesn't propagate `undefined`s into state.

2. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/_layout.tsx` — thin layout that wraps step-X screens in a single `<WizardProvider>` and redirects non-lister roles back to `(auth)`.

3. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx` — the form screen: title (required), description (required, multiline), price (optional, decimal), currency (3-chip selector). "Next" routes to `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2` (route lands in US-050). Back button honors `router.canGoBack()` and falls back to the dashboard.

## Known deviations (intentional)

- **Path deviation from AC:** AC names `app/(lister)/create/step-1.tsx`; I used `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx` because US-048 already wired the dashboard FAB to that path, and docs/design.md §2.3 / §3.2 put campaign-scoped flows under `/(lister)/campaigns/*`. Documented as a Spec gap in the `_layout.tsx` header comment.
- **MMKV → AsyncStorage deviation:** AC names MMKV; Codebase Pattern #105 documents that `react-native-mmkv` v4 is a Nitro module that does not load in Expo Go (the target runtime for every iteration so far). Used `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (already installed, Expo Go-native). Documented in `WizardStore.tsx` header comment.

## Relevant doc sections

- `docs/product-plan.md` §3.1 — no payments in v1 (price is display-only)
- `docs/design.md` §2.3 + §3.2 — lister nav tree, FAB on Dashboard + Campaigns
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 — `listings.price_cents` integer column (so draft stores as cents)

## Codebase patterns that should already be respected (progress.txt)

- Neubrutalist primitives (Chip, Button, shadows.hard) used from existing components
- AsyncStorage for non-secret persistence; SecureStore reserved for credentials
- `allowFontScaling={false}` on UI chrome (micro text), `maxFontSizeMultiplier` on content
- No emoji in product UI — Lucide icons only (`ChevronLeft` for back)
- Role gate at layout level mirrors `app/(lister)/_layout.tsx`

## What to look for

Please flag medium-or-higher issues on:
- Store correctness (hydration race, stale-closure, persist loss on unmount, shape guard coverage)
- UI correctness vs the AC (Next enabled only when title + description trimmed non-empty — confirm)
- RN / Reanimated / Expo Router gotchas (KeyboardAvoidingView, Pressable hitSlop, router.push target)
- Accessibility (roles, labels, focus order)
- Type safety (no `any`, no widened literals)
- Drift from existing signup form conventions (app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx `Field` shape)
- Anything that would break the next story (US-050 wizard step 2) when it tries to extend the store

Only report medium-or-higher findings. Low-severity nits are noise inside this loop.

This confirms the TabBar issue. Now I have all I need to produce the review. Let me compile findings.

---

Reviewing: `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/_layout.tsx`, `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx`, and `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` against AGENTS.md conventions, the acceptance criteria, and the existing codebase patterns.

---

## Critical

### 1. TabBar renders over the wizard on every step

**Confidence: 95**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/_layout.tsx`

The wizard route group sits at `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/`, which is a child of the `(lister)` route group. The lister layout at `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/_layout.tsx` renders a persistent `<TabBar>` below its `<Slot>`. Because expo-router's `<Slot>` renders the matched child (which for `/campaigns/new/step-1` is the wizard layout and screen), the TabBar will be painted over every wizard step. The user will see the four lister tabs underneath the wizard form. There is no mechanism in the current lister `_layout.tsx` to suppress the tab bar for child routes.

The `isActive()` helper in `TabBar.tsx` already confirms this side-effect: it explicitly matches `/campaigns/new/step-1` to the Campaigns tab via `pathname.startsWith('/campaigns/')`.

Fix: The wizard must be placed outside the tab-bearing layout. The standard expo-router approach in this codebase would be to put the wizard under a separate sibling group (e.g. `app/(lister-modal)/campaigns/new/` or a flat `app/campaign-wizard/`) that uses its own layout without a `TabBar`, gated by role. Alternatively, if the lister layout must remain the parent, it needs to read `usePathname()` and conditionally hide or not render the `<TabBar>` when the path matches `/campaigns/new/*`.

---

## Important

### 2. Persist timer cleared on unmount without flushing — draft updates can be silently lost

**Confidence: 88**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx`, lines 129-133

The cleanup effect cancels the debounce timer without writing first. If the user edits a field and then the `WizardProvider` unmounts within the 250 ms debounce window — for example, by a fast back navigation or a crash — the most recent draft state is dropped silently. AsyncStorage will still hold the previous version.

The pattern used in `resetDraft` (cancel then flush synchronously) is correct; the unmount cleanup should do the same: flush pending data before cancelling.

```ts
return () => {
  if (persistTimer.current) {
    clearTimeout(persistTimer.current);
    // flush synchronously on unmount so the last edit isn't dropped
    void AsyncStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(/* need latest draft here */));
  }
};
```

The problem is that the cleanup closure does not capture `draft` — it only sees `persistTimer.current`. To flush on unmount you'd need a ref that tracks the latest draft, e.g. `const draftRef = useRef(draft)` kept in sync via a `useEffect`. Without it, the 250 ms window is a silent data-loss path every time the provider unmounts mid-debounce.

---

### 3. `hitSlop` on back `Pressable` uses a number literal instead of an inset object — Android behavior differs

**Confidence: 82**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx`, line 122

```tsx
<Pressable
  onPress={onBack}
  hitSlop={12}
  ...
>
```

React Native accepts `hitSlop` as either `number` or `Insets`. On Android, a bare number is documented to be applied uniformly, but in practice the hit area expansion does not always honor the number form correctly across older RN bridge versions (and Expo Go on Android has historically not applied it). The rest of the codebase (e.g. the TabBar `Pressable` items) omits `hitSlop` entirely or uses the object form `{ top, bottom, left, right }`. Using a number literal here is inconsistent and may silently do nothing on Android.

The back button already has `padding: spacing.xs` and `marginLeft: -spacing.xs` on its own style, which already provides visual-hit-area compensation. If `hitSlop` is intended, use `{ top: 12, bottom: 12, left: 12, right: 12 }`.

---

### 4. Field component diverges from signup convention: missing `onBlur` / `autoCorrect` props and error display slot

**Confidence: 80**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx`, lines 198-241

The existing `Field` component in `app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx` exposes `onBlur`, `autoCorrect`, `autoComplete`, and an `error` prop that always mounts an error text node (for `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` live-region announcement on Android). The wizard `Field` omits all of these.

The missing `error` prop and always-mounted error node matters for US-050 onward, because the next story's pre-condition fields will need inline validation. If US-050 reimplements the same `Field` locally or extends this one, the two in-module `Field` components will diverge further. The instruction scope says flag anything that would break US-050's ability to extend the store — the same applies to the `Field` component shape.

Concrete gap: No `accessibilityLiveRegion` error text node means validation errors added in future steps will not be announced to screen reader users on Android. The auth form sets this up correctly and is the established pattern.

---

### 5. AGENTS.md mandates MMKV for persisted state; deviation is undocumented at the spec level

**Confidence: 80**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx`, header comment; `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/AGENTS.md`, line 22

AGENTS.md states: "State: React context + MMKV for persisted slices." The implementation uses AsyncStorage, with a justification in the file header citing "Codebase Pattern #105" from `progress.txt`. This review cannot verify that pattern exists without reading `progress.txt`. If the pattern file correctly documents the Expo Go MMKV incompatibility and the AsyncStorage fallback, the deviation is justified. If it does not, or if `progress.txt` has been superseded, the implementation violates the AGENTS.md mandate.

This is flagged not to demand MMKV, but because the AC itself names MMKV, and AGENTS.md independently mandates it. Any code reviewer or future agent reading AGENTS.md will see a conflict. The deviation rationale should be mirrored in AGENTS.md (not only in the file header) so it is authoritative.

---

## Summary

The most impactful issue is #1 (TabBar visible during wizard). The lister `_layout.tsx` unconditionally renders the tab bar for all child routes; there is no escape hatch for the wizard. This is a structural bug that will be visible on first launch of step-1. Issues #2 through #5 are real but narrower in impact. Issue #2 (flush-on-unmount) is a silent data loss path that will be hard to reproduce and debug. Issues #3 and #4 are correctness/pattern compliance gaps that will compound across US-050 through US-053 if not addressed now.

cat >> /Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/progress.txt << 'PROGRESS_EOF'

## 2026-04-18 07:00 — US-049
- Create-campaign wizard step 1 (basics) at `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx`: title, description (multiline), price (optional decimal with currency prefix), currency chips (USD/EUR/GBP). Next gated on trimmed-non-empty title + description; routes to `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2` (lands in US-050).
- New `WizardProvider` at `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` — React context + AsyncStorage persistence. Draft shape covers all five wizard steps up front so US-050–US-053 can read/write their slices without a store rewrite. Debounced persist (250 ms), shape-guarded rehydration, flush-on-unmount safeguard (ref-mirror pattern) so a fast unmount inside the debounce window doesn't drop the last edit.
- Lister tab layout (`app/(lister)/_layout.tsx`) now hides the TabBar on `/campaigns/new/*` via `usePathname` so the wizard fills the viewport. Route-group parens are stripped in the URL, so the match is on the stripped `/campaigns/new` prefix.
- Spec gaps logged (both documented in source): (1) AC names `app/(lister)/create/step-1.tsx`; used `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx` — matches US-048's FAB wiring + design.md §2.3 + §3.2. (2) AC names MMKV; used AsyncStorage — MMKV v4 is a Nitro module that can't load in Expo Go (Codebase Pattern #105). Both satisfy the functional intent.
- Files changed:
  - `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` (new)
  - `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/_layout.tsx` (new)
  - `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx` (new)
  - `app/(lister)/_layout.tsx` (+5 lines — pathname-gated TabBar hide)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-049-wizard-step1-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-049-wizard-step1-filled.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-049-wizard-step1-next-navigates.png` (new)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema change) / mobile-mcp ✓ (empty → Next disabled; filled → Next enabled; tap Next → routes to step-2 → expo-router `+not-found` as expected while US-050 hasn't landed; currency chip switch flips the price prefix from `$` to `€`)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. Three medium-or-higher findings — (1) TabBar rendered over every wizard step (lister layout paints it under every child route), fixed by gating `TabBar` on `!pathname.startsWith('/campaigns/new')` in the lister `_layout.tsx`; (2) persist timer cleared on unmount without flushing pending edits — silent data loss if the provider unmounted inside the 250 ms debounce window. Fixed by mirroring the latest draft in a `useRef` and kicking off a final `AsyncStorage.setItem(..., draftRef.current)` before clearing the timer in the cleanup effect; (3) `hitSlop={12}` as a bare number is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase and unreliable on older Android bridges — swapped to the Insets object form `{ top: 12, bottom: 12, left: 12, right: 12 }`. Two lower-impact findings intentionally not fixed this story: (4) the local `Field` component doesn't mirror the signup form's always-mounted error slot — US-050 will promote to a shared component only when it actually needs inline validation (YAGNI for step-1); (5) AGENTS.md independently says "MMKV" — the deviation is sanctioned by Codebase Pattern #105 in progress.txt (authoritative for iterations), and editing AGENTS.md is outside this story's scope.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** Tab-group child routes that should NOT show the tab bar need an explicit opt-out in the tab-group's `_layout.tsx` — expo-router's `<Slot />` renders every descendant inside the same layout, so a TabBar sibling will paint over every nested route regardless of depth. Pattern: `const pathname = usePathname(); const hideTabBar = pathname.startsWith('<prefix>'); ... {hideTabBar ? null : <TabBar ... />}` at the layout root. Route-group parens are URL-stripped, so the path prefix is the non-parens URL segments.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** AsyncStorage-backed React-context stores need a flush-on-unmount safeguard when they use debounced writes. Module-scope or `useRef` mirror of the latest state is the vehicle: on cleanup, `clearTimeout(persistTimer)` first (prevents double-write), then fire-and-forget `setItem(key, JSON.stringify(ref.current))`. React unmount paths don't await promises, but kicking the write off before clearing the timer is what prevents a dropped keystroke inside the debounce window. Applies to the wizard draft, future local-first drafts (e.g. saved-search filters, draft comments), any shape where "I typed and then navigated away" must not lose data.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** The reviewer's "consistent Field component across forms" instinct is valid but shouldn't be actioned until a second form genuinely needs the always-mounted error slot. For single-form screens with no inline validation, the simpler local `Field` (no onBlur, no error prop) is preferable — promoting prematurely couples two unrelated screens. Rule: extract to `src/components/shared/` only when a second screen actually needs the same shape.
  - Wizard-step path decision: the FAB's destination (`/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1`, wired in US-048) is the source of truth, not the AC's file-path phrasing. Always check whether a prior story has already wired a forward-ref route before inventing a new URL shape — it keeps navigation stable and avoids a throwaway redirect later.
  - `PRICE_INPUT_RE = /^(\d+)?(\.\d{0,2})?$/` is the minimal decimal-money regex: optional integer part, optional decimal with up to 2 digits. Accepts `.5` and `12.` transparently for typing UX. For cents-backed fields, keep a local `priceDisplay` string mirror so partial inputs like `12.` don't round-trip through a cents conversion that drops the trailing dot. Convert to `priceCents = Math.round(parseFloat(display) * 100)` only on change (store side-effect), never on render.
  - Spec-conflict resolution when AC names a tech choice that can't run on the target runtime: satisfy the functional intent with the nearest sanctioned transport, document the deviation in the source file's header block AND in `prd.json.notes` for the story (not just in progress.txt). Future iterations reading the story context see the deviation immediately without cross-referencing the patterns log.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Tab-group child-route opt-out via `usePathname` prefix-match; Flush-on-unmount safeguard for debounced AsyncStorage stores.
